comment_text.sn8.6:4.3: Vaṅgīsa emphasizes the aesthetic and emotional qualities of speech.

root_text.sn8.6:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sāriputto sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn8.6:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā.
root_text.sn8.6:1.3: Te ca bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.sn8.6:1.5: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā.
root_text.sn8.6:1.6: Te ca bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.sn8.6:1.7: Yannūnāhaṁ āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthaveyyan”ti.
root_text.sn8.6:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yenāyasmā sāriputto tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn8.6:2.2: “paṭibhāti maṁ, āvuso sāriputta, paṭibhāti maṁ, āvuso sāriputtā”ti.
root_text.sn8.6:2.4: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:

translation_text.sn8.6:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn8.6:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Sāriputta was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk. His words were polished, clear, articulate, and expressed the meaning.
translation_text.sn8.6:1.3: And those mendicants were paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.sn8.6:1.5: “This Venerable Sāriputta is educating the mendicants. …
translation_text.sn8.6:1.6: And those mendicants are paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.sn8.6:1.7: Why don’t I extoll him in his presence with fitting verses?”
translation_text.sn8.6:2.1: Then Venerable Vaṅgīsa got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward Sāriputta, and said,
translation_text.sn8.6:2.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Reverend Sāriputta! I feel inspired to speak, Reverend Sāriputta!”
translation_text.sn8.6:2.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said Sāriputta.
translation_text.sn8.6:2.4: Then Vaṅgīsa extolled Sāriputta in his presence with fitting verses:
translation_text.sn8.6:3.2: expert in the path and what is not the path;
translation_text.sn8.6:4.3: His call, like a myna bird,
translation_text.sn8.6:5.2: the mendicants listen to his sweet voice,

variant_text.sn8.6:1.2: anelagalāya → aneḷagalāya (bj, pts2ed, mr); anelagaḷāya (sya-all, km, pts1ed)

reference_text.sn8.6:1.1: dr15.264, ms12S1_1331, msdiv214, ndp12.190, pts-vp-pli1ed1.190, pts-vp-pli2ed1.409, sya15.279, vri23.220
